title: Opinions on RoE 20
wikitext:
[]: Opinions on RoE 2.0             Quote Post by simon » Mon Jul 13, 2009 3:23 am So I haven't seen the movie yet, and i'm not going to watch it until it's available on DVD. However i'd like to hear opinions on the movie, but all the threads are tagged with spoiler alerts. That's why I ask you here to state your opinions without any inside info on the movie. I know it's not easy, but try to express your feelings on the movie, your opinions on how things were done in general, without any spoilers. Did they put too many angels? did the graphics satisfy you? and so on... GENERAL ANSWERS ONLY. NO SPOILERS UNDER ANY CONDITION IN THIS THREAD. NOT EVEN MARKED, WHITE COLOURED OR HIDDEN IN ANY OTHER WAY . My will is weak so I wouldn't be able to stop myself from revealing it. Thanks in advance for your cooperation and good luck :)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Jul 13, 2009 5:17 am From what little I've seen, the imagery is pretty much like 1.0, the music is excellent, and the battles are astounding. I found whatever I watched entertaining, and I'm sure that you'll find the full movie real fun to watch. Ah, yes...don't expect it to conform to the original series. 2.0 is a very different animal, for the better or the worse.

[]:             Quote Post by Barong » Mon Jul 13, 2009 8:13 am I liked the bits we got to see thus far very much. It sure is action-packed and I bet the rest of the movie will be, too, since there are plenty of angels to fight. Like Ursus said, visuals and soundtrack are astounding. Sometimes all the effects make it seem quite a bit overloaded, but I guess that's the way it's done nowadays and would bet my butt that, if GAINAX hadn't been short of money most of the time, the original series' action scenes would've looked very similar. I found the new story-elements introduced very intriguing and can't wait to find out what they are all about. Actually it's a necessity for me to wait and see how those loose ends turn out to be dealt with before finally rating 2.0. Let's put it this way: As an independent movie 2.0 is a visually extraordinary action fest. As a part of a trilogy/quadrilogy of feature length movies - in retrospective - it'll most likely be considered a great set-up for the coming final act(s) of Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 13, 2009 9:16 am All I can say is so far it sounds/seems amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Mon Jul 13, 2009 9:31 am I thought the visuals were amazing,and that was just from a sucky camrip.The soundtrack is equally amazing.From what I heard I think that the movie keeps enough in that it still feels like evangelion,but changes enough to keep you from predicting whats going to happen next.

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Mon Jul 13, 2009 10:05 am Too much liberty-taking that doesn't make sense even for an AU imo tbh fyi.

[]:             Quote Post by JCunningham02 » Mon Jul 13, 2009 10:08 am I'm really impressed with what I've seen so far. It doesn't have as much depth or character development as the original series (so far at least), but as a stand alone film it works very well. As long as you think of it as its own beast and not a straight up remake, you should enjoy it, if for nothing more than the animation and action alone.

[]:             Quote Post by evapilot » Mon Jul 13, 2009 9:20 pm It's incredible. It's what I'd want an Evangelion film to be now , fourteen years later. And while the action is impressive, what impressed me the most were the beautiful, organic renderings of the natural environment. I thought of Ghibli several times. And unlike 1.0 finally every character in every scene is in true Rebuild style. I like that. I think everyone looks better, more "real," more solid/physical, more human.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jul 13, 2009 11:09 pm ^Quick note- That's nice to hear. One of the problems in 1.0 is the characters fluctuated in drawing style. It doesn't bother me in the TV series but in a film it was a little distracting to see Shinji go from cherubic animation to sleek animation that looks like Sadamoto's drawings. As for 2.0, of course I'm excited beyond all reason and I've everything I've heard about the film so far only heightens my anticipation.

[]:             Quote Post by snowballreborn » Tue Jul 14, 2009 12:54 am the only bad things about the film is the soundtrack is hit and miss and the ending is a real "no u" experience

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Tue Jul 14, 2009 9:40 am What about Asuka? How much did they change her? Is she still so self-centered, ill-natured frustrated kid?

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Jul 14, 2009 9:49 am From what I have heard thus far "Asuka" is a lot less..How can I say this..Timid. I assume instead of ANTA BAKA! its more Anta Baka

[]:             Quote Post by Shackel » Tue Jul 14, 2009 10:27 am I hate Mari. That's as far as I can go without going "Massive Spoiler".
